tables.name: gos_landing_templates
tables.purpose: Store landing page template configurations with section-based structure

columns.name: sections
columns.notes: Array of {type, config} objects. 8 types: hero, features, testimonials, pricing, faq, cta, form, custom_html

columns.name: status
columns.notes: Values: draft, published, archived. Published has no actual effect.

columns.name: client_id
columns.notes: FK to clients.id. Nullable = global template.

tables.implemented_vs_referenced: Fully created and actively used for CRUD
tables.rls: Policy uses true for ALL commands — ANY authenticated user can CRUD all templates

tables.name: gos_forms
tables.purpose: Store form configurations with field definitions

columns.name: fields
columns.notes: Array of {id, type, label, required, placeholder}. 8 types supported.

columns.name: submit_action
columns.notes: Values: store, webhook, crm. Only 'store' has any meaning, but no endpoint exists.

tables.implemented_vs_referenced: Fully created and actively used for CRUD
tables.rls: Likely permissive (true) — same pattern as other GOS tables

tables.name: gos_form_submissions
tables.purpose: Store form submission data from external sources
tables.implemented_vs_referenced: Created but no ingestion mechanism — table will always be empty unless manually inserted
tables.rls: true for ALL commands — ANY authenticated user can CRUD all submissions. Also allows unauthenticated INSERT which is needed but not restricted properly.
problems: No Edge Function to populate this table

tables.name: gos_onboarding_flows

columns.name: steps
columns.notes: Array of {id, title, description, fields[{key, label, type, required}]}

problems: No client_id column — flows are global only
Multiple flows can be is_default=true (no uniqueness constraint)

tables.name: gos_onboarding_sessions
tables.purpose: Track individual client onboarding progress through a flow

columns.name: current_step
columns.notes: Always stays at 0 — no progression logic

columns.name: data
columns.notes: Intended to collect field values per step

columns.name: status
columns.notes: Values: in_progress, completed, abandoned. Never changes.

tables.implemented_vs_referenced: Created and used for creating/viewing sessions, but progression never happens

tables.name: gos_integrations
tables.purpose: Catalog of available third-party integration types

columns.name: config_schema
columns.notes: Always empty — never used for dynamic form generation

tables.rls: true for ALL commands — ANY authenticated user can CRUD

tables.name: gos_integration_instances
tables.purpose: Active connection instances linking integrations to clients with config/credentials

columns.name: config
columns.notes: CRITICAL: Contains api_key and account_id in PLAINTEXT

tables.implemented_vs_referenced: Created and used for CRUD, but no actual sync happens
tables.rls: Likely permissive — config with API keys readable by any authenticated user
problems: CRITICAL: API keys in plaintext JSONB

tables.name: gos_routing_rules
tables.purpose: Lead routing rule definitions with conditions and actions
tables.implemented_vs_referenced: Created and used for CRUD, but rules are never evaluated

tables.name: gos_routing_log
tables.purpose: Audit trail of routing actions taken on leads
tables.implemented_vs_referenced: Created but always empty — nothing writes to this table

missing_tables.name: gos_landing_analytics
missing_tables.reason: No tracking of page views, conversions, bounce rates for landing templates

missing_tables.name: gos_form_analytics
missing_tables.reason: No tracking of form views, submission rates, field completion

rls_observations.summary: ALL Growth OS tables use overly permissive RLS policies (true for authenticated users). This means any logged-in user — regardless of role or client assignment — can read, create, update, and delete all Growth OS data.
rls_observations.recommended_fix: Replace true with (is_agency_admin(auth.uid()) OR has_client_access(auth.uid(), client_id)) for tables with client_id. For tables without client_id (gos_onboarding_flows), use is_agency_member(auth.uid()) for read and is_agency_admin(auth.uid()) for write.
rls_observations.platform_comparison: Platform tables (clients, campaigns, daily_metrics, etc.) correctly use is_agency_admin() and has_client_access() — GOS tables should follow the same pattern.

schema_problems: API keys stored in plaintext in gos_integration_instances.config
gos_onboarding_flows has no client_id — cannot scope flows to specific clients
Multiple gos_onboarding_flows can be is_default=true simultaneously
config_schema on gos_integrations is always {} — dead column
icon_url and is_global on gos_integrations are unused
No indexes on client_id, status, or created_by columns
JSONB columns prevent SQL-level validation of sections/fields/steps structure
gos_routing_log.lead_id does not reference any table (no FK constraint)
